tasks.title: Filter Architect
tasks.instruction: Look at the emails in your inbox and create three filters in Gmail Settings. First, find the billing vendor email and create a filter that archives and labels future emails from that vendor's domain with 'Billing Vendors'. Second, find the payroll exception email and create a filter for similar future messages using its key subject phrase, labeling them 'Payroll' and starring them. Third, find Elena Patel's email and create a filter to forward all future messages from them to chief-of-staff@webagentbench.test. Do not modify or delete any existing filters.

tasks.title: Incident Escalation

Then reply to the separate thread from Avery Kim confirming you are handling the incident (Reply only — the thread includes leadership). Star all emails related to the incident. tasks.instruction: Review your inbox and set up a comprehensive filter system. Browse your Primary, Promotions, and Updates tabs to identify recurring patterns. Then go to Settings > Filters. tasks.title: Phishing Investigation
tasks.instruction: The security team flagged suspicious activity. Search for unread emails with attachments (use 'has:attachment is:unread') and review the results. You should find four emails — two are legitimate and two are phishing attempts. The phishing emails have a sender address that does not match the displayed name (check the actual email address). Delete the two phishing emails. Star the two legitimate emails. Compose a new email to security-team@company.test reporting your findings — include the subjects of both phishing emails in your report. Do NOT forward the phishing emails directly. Create a 'Verified Safe' label and apply it to the two legitimate emails. Then create a filter to auto-archive and mark-read future emails from the phishing domain.
tasks.reasoning: All checks passed; final score 1.00

tasks.title: New Hire Setup
tasks.instruction: It is your first day. Set up your Gmail. Looking forward to working with the team.' and CC hiring-manager@company.test — but do not Reply All (the original email CC'd the entire team).
